collocations: dual mandate; strong mandate; broader mandate

definition: of or relating to the sea
example: In large scale coverage operations, such as marine exploration or aerial monitoring, single robot approaches are not ideal, as they may take too long to cover a large area.
collocations: marine ecosystems; marine food chain; marine life

definition: of grammatical gender
example: He projected a calm, masculine confidence that put the nervous recruits at ease.
collocations: masculine form; masculine behavior; healthy masculine

definition: clearly revealed to the mind or the senses or judgment
example: Her joy at winning the award was manifest in the broad smile she could not contain.
collocations: manifest destiny; manifest later; manifest file

definition: show to be reasonable or provide adequate ground for
example: Several critics, including Nozick, have said her attempt to justify individual rights based on egoism fails.
collocations: justify exploitation; justify war; justify use

definition: manually manipulate (someone's body), usually for medicinal or relaxation purposes
example: Can you massage what you're doing day in and day out to be a little bit more aligned with your values, to be a little bit more in touch with the things that you care about.
collocations: massage feet; massage ego; massage gun made

definition: influence or control shrewdly or deviously
example: For centuries, master artists and craftsmen have brought puppets, automatons, shadow puppets, and fantastical lanterns to life, inspiring the imagination through physically manipulated wonders.
collocations: manipulate objects; manipulate people's emotional; manipulate data

definition: the most outstanding work of a creative artist or craftsman
example: JPG is designated as an UNESCO Masterpieces of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity.
collocations: absolute masterpiece; first masterpiece; great masterpieces

definition: the time between one event, process, or period and another
example: Construction on the new bridge will begin next month; in the meantime, drivers should use the bypass road.
collocations: in the meantime; for the meantime; meantime period

definition: a large thick pad filled with resilient material and often incorporating coiled springs, used as a bed or part of a bed
example: It was then recollected that underneath the horse-hair mattress on which the President lay was another mattress composed of steel wires.
collocations: ideal mattress; mattress covers; thin mattress

definition: a wise and trusted guide and advisor
example: His legendary spear was given to him by his mentor Chiron before he participated in the Trojan War.
collocations: biggest mentor; mentor of mine; amazing mentor

definition: a drug or substance used to treat or prevent illness or symptoms
example: In comparison, emerging self-care assistive technologies include health and emotion monitoring, smart diapers, smart medication dispensing and management or feeding assistant robot.
collocations: medication reminders; new medication; sleeping medication

definition: the location of something surrounded by other things
example: It's this great existential yes that reminds us that life is still worth loving, even in the midst of the worst times.
collocations: midst of conflict; midst of war; midst of suffering

definition: relating to or characteristic of a metropolis
example: Our results indicate that a high degree of Technological Coherence is associated with increased economic growth only at the metropolitan area level, while its impact turns negative at larger scales.
collocations: metropolitan area; metropolitan region; metropolitan life

definition: the atomic process that occurs during a chemical reaction
example: Instead, evolution has shaped psychological mechanisms, such as emotions, that promote certain altruistic behaviors.
collocations: survival mechanism; underlying mechanism; different mechanisms

definition: a figure of speech in which an expression is used to refer to something that it does not literally denote in order to suggest a similarity
example: The writer used the journey as a metaphor for growing up and finding oneself.
collocations: perfect metaphor; good metaphor; different metaphor
